assertions.text: The vehicle was accelerating eastbound prior to the collision.
The Accord was rear-ended from behind by another vehicle (impact from the rear).
The driver braked at the same time as the rear impact occurred.
Acceleration occurred before the rear impact.
A specific travel speed is not stated (no exact speed claimed).
There was a single rear impact (one collision event).
No explicit severity description was given (severity unspecified).

verdicts.evidence: The speed increased from [5.0 km/h](T2) at t=-5.0 s to [35.0 km/h](T2) at t=0.0 s showing acceleration prior to impact; the accelerator pedal baseline median was [21.0 %](T2) consistent with throttle input.

The crash pulse shows a negative longitudinal delta-V with a final value of [−18.0 km/h](T2) and a peak at [−18.0 km/h](T1) at [142.5 ms](T1) after t=0, indicating the vehicle experienced a rearward-decoupling style delta-V consistent with being struck from behind (SAE sign: negative for deceleration into forward-facing impact).

Brake channel shows a transition from off to on at [t=−0.5 s](T2) and the last pre-impact brake sample is [1](T2) at t=0.0 s, indicating the driver applied the brakes immediately before or around impact time.

Speed rose from [5.0 km/h](T2) at t=[−5.0 s](T2) to [35.0 km/h](T2) at t=[0.0 s](T2) (acceleration) prior to the impact at t=0, while the crash pulse begins at t=0 with longitudinal delta-V reaching [−18.0 km/h](T2), so acceleration occurred before impact.
No exact travel speed was claimed; the telemetry shows pre-impact speed samples up to [35.0 km/h](T2) but the statement contains no specific numeric speed.

EDR summary reports [num_events_recorded: 1](T1) and the delta-V time series shows a single cumulative longitudinal pulse reaching [−18.0 km/h](T2) by the end of the crash pulse, consistent with one impact.

The narrative gave no explicit severity metric; telemetry shows a maximum longitudinal delta-V magnitude of [18.0 km/h](T2) but the assertion that severity was unspecified is consistent with there being no severity descriptor in the narrative.

result.rationale: Telemetry shows the vehicle was accelerating before t=0 ([5.0 km/h → 35.0 km/h](T2)), experienced a single crash pulse with longitudinal delta-V [−18.0 km/h](T2) consistent with a rear impact, and a brake application at [t=−0.5 s](T2) immediately prior to impact; all narrative assertions are supported by the sensors.

result.time_zero: impact (t=0); pre-crash negative, crash pulse positive

channels.name: delta_v_long
channels.desc: Cumulative longitudinal delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

channels.name: delta_v_lat
channels.desc: Cumulative lateral delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

result.notes: NHTSA CISS EDR data: sparse pre-crash series (~2 Hz, t<0) plus a crash-pulse delta-V curve (t>=0, ~10 ms steps). t=0 is the impact.

accel_pedal.integrated_delta_v_note: integral of (a - baseline) over the range; device-frame axis, sign/orientation not vehicle-aligned

delta_v_long.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

delta_v_lat.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse
